js_take.en-US: You’re not selling automation—you’re selling the job of babysitting it. Real money is in building systems that run while you sleep, not invoices for setup.
js_take.zh-CN: 你卖的不是自动化，而是'伺候自动化'的差事。真正赚钱的是能24小时自动运转的系统，而不是一次性的搭建服务费。

js_take.en-US: 'Chewing glass for 4 weeks' isn't grit—it's Stockholm syndrome with your stack. The tools that win won't demand suffering as cover charge.
js_take.zh-CN: 「啃玻璃啃了4周」不是毅力，是你对技术栈的斯德哥尔摩综合征。真正好用的工具不会把受苦当门票。

js_take.en-US: Cursor building OpenClaw isn't a flex—it's defensive panic. The war isn't about feature lists; it's about who removes the most decisions from devs' plates. Fewer choices, stickier moat.

js_take.en-US: Sold out Macs aren't demand overflow—it's timing validating itself. The best marketing isn't ads, it's making people want what they can't have.
js_take.zh-CN: Mac售罄不是需求爆棚，是时机在自我证明。最好的营销不是广告，而是让人求而不得。

top_items.author: @cobie

title.en-US: OpenClaw sent 50k invoices to Fortune500, 2% paid without checking
title.zh-CN: OpenClaw 群发 5 万张发票，财富 500 强 2% 未核即付

summary.zh-CN: OpenClaw、自动化、商业模式

top_items.relevance: OpenClaw / 自动化 / 商业模式

js_take.en-US: You didn't find a bug—you found the margin of error built into scale. That 2% isn't carelessness, it's the cost of trust at Fortune 500 size. Automation just makes the leakage systematic.
js_take.zh-CN: 你发现的根本不是bug，而是规模扩张必然带来的误差空间。那2%不是粗心，是500强体量的信任成本。自动化只是让这种泄漏变得系统性而已。

top_items.author: @levelsio

title.en-US: Ran OpenClaw for a month with 26 friends, made a self-improving game
title.zh-CN: 26人协作运行OpenClaw一月，打造自进化游戏

js_take.en-US: Everyone's dissecting the code. But 26 people shaping one agent? That's not a tech demo—it's a fight for whose instincts win. The real story isn't what it built. It's whose brain it ended up thinking like.
js_take.zh-CN: 所有人都在拆解代码分析技术。但26个人共同塑造一个agent？这不是技术演示——而是关于谁的直觉能胜出的博弈。真正的故事不是它造出了什么，而是它最终学会了像谁一样思考。

top_items.author: @TheRundownAI

title.en-US: Jensen Huang: OpenClaw is the most important software release ever, surpassing Linux's 30-year adoption in months
title.zh-CN: 黄仁勋：OpenClaw超越Linux三十年积累，成史上最重要软件发布

js_take.en-US: "30x faster than Linux" isn't a product win—it's timing. Linux spent 30 years waiting for a world that didn't need it. OpenClaw just showed up when everyone was already starving.
js_take.zh-CN: "比Linux快30倍"不是产品胜利，而是时机。Linux花了30年等待一个不需要它的世界，OpenClaw却在众人饥渴时恰好出现。

top_items.author: @Hesamation

title.en-US: Gave OpenClaw a body and saw it take its first breath

summary.en-US: OpenClaw 物理 embodiment，情感共鸣强的话题

js_take.en-US: Intelligence isn’t in the hardware or the code—it’s in the friction between them. We don’t build robots to mimic life; we build them to buy ourselves time to actually live.
js_take.zh-CN: 智能不在硬件里，也不在代码里——而在两者之间的摩擦中。我们造机器人不是为了模仿生命，而是为了给自己争取真正去生活的时间。

top_items.author: @ErickSky

title.en-US: Jordanian dev with GTX 1060 built Rust Agent OS that outperformed OpenClaw
title.zh-CN: 约旦开发者凭GTX 1060打造Rust Agent OS，性能超越OpenClaw

js_take.en-US: Arguing Rust vs OpenClaw misses the point. The real question: do you want to become the kind of builder who values raw precision, or the kind who ships fast and iterates? Your tool choice is your identity. Pick wisely.
js_take.zh-CN: 争论Rust还是OpenClaw其实没抓住重点。真正的问题在于：你想成为追求极致精确的builder，还是快速交付、持续迭代的builder？工具选择就是你的身份认同。慎重选择。

js_take.zh-CN: 774倍速突破 Cloudflare。很酷。现在你有774倍的数据，却不知道该怎么用了。没有判断力的速度，不过是更快的噪音。

top_items.author: @Pirat_Nation

title.en-US: NVIDIA CEO says OpenClaw did in 3 weeks what Linux took 30 years to achieve
title.zh-CN: NVIDIA CEO称OpenClaw用3周做到了Linux 30年才达到的成就，称其为史上最重要的软件发布。

summary.en-US: OpenClaw核心话题，AI Agent基础设施里程碑
summary.zh-CN: 做纠正： Linux的30年是生态沉淀，OpenClaw的3周是 adoption 速度，两者本质不同却被混为一谈。真正的洞见是——当工具足够贴近用户需求，传播不需要时间，需要的是对的时机。

top_items.relevance: OpenClaw核心话题，AI Agent基础设施里程碑

js_take.en-US: False comparison. Linux spent 30 years building a philosophy; OpenClaw proved the philosophy was already there waiting for a tool that actually works. Speed isn't the story—utility is.
js_take.zh-CN: 类比不当。Linux 花了 30 年才沉淀出一套哲学；而 OpenClaw 证明，这套理念其实一直都在，只是在等待一个真正好用的工具。重要的不是速度，而是实用价值。

top_items.author: @tpschmidt_

title.zh-CN: 把 OpenClaw 从本地移到 AWS Lightsail $5 实例上运行，解决了机器休眠问题，效果很好

summary.en-US: OpenClaw 部署实践、Agent 基础设施

top_items.relevance: OpenClaw 部署实践、Agent 基础设施

js_take.en-US: AWS doesn't sleep, but neither does its billing. You're not choosing infrastructure—you're picking which 3am panic you prefer: waking up to a dead box, or a $200 surprise.
js_take.zh-CN: AWS 全年无休，账单也是。你选的不是基础设施，而是凌晨3点的惊魂模式——服务器宕机，还是200刀账单突袭。

title.en-US: OpenClaw benchmark discussion

summary.en-US: OpenClaw 平台 + Agent 工具链
summary.zh-CN: 做纠正：大家在争论'哪个模型最好用'，但这问错了问题。真正决定 agent 表现的从不是模型本身，而是使用者的判断力——你给什么上下文、怎么拆解任务、何时该停手。AI 的上限就是使用 AI 的人。

top_items.relevance: OpenClaw 平台 + Agent 工具链

js_take.en-US: You're fighting over which wrench is best while building the wrong house. OpenClaw's ceiling was never the model—it was always the hands holding it.
js_take.zh-CN: 你们在为哪把扳手更好吵得不可开交，却浑然不觉正在盖一栋错误的房子。OpenClaw 的天花板从来不是模型——而是握着它的那双手。

top_items.author: @altryne

title.en-US: GitHub stopped counting OpenClaw issues at 5K+
title.zh-CN: OpenClaw的GitHub issue数量太多，GitHub干脆不数了，直接显示"5K+"

summary.en-US: OpenClaw平台热度，开发者生态
summary.zh-CN: 做纠正：人们以为爆红靠营销，但真正的产品增长是"被需要"的自然结果——不是你在追用户，是用户在追你。

top_items.relevance: OpenClaw平台热度，开发者生态

js_take.en-US: GitHub's counter didn't break from bugs. It broke because OpenClaw never chased growth—it just got chased. The best products don't scale; they get pulled.
js_take.zh-CN: GitHub计数器崩掉不是因为bug，是因为OpenClaw从不追增长——而是被增长追着跑。最好的产品不是「扩张」出来的，是「被拉」过去的。

top_items.author: @cathrynlavery

title.en-US: OpenClaw skill libraries add startup overhead due to upfront discovery
title.zh-CN: 使用OpenClaw时发现大型skill库会增加启动开销，因为skill发现是前置发生的——即使本次会话不会用到这些skill。

summary.en-US: 直接讨论OpenClaw平台的技术细节，与JS的核心项目强相关
summary.zh-CN: 把skill发现比作'月旦评'——汉末名士见面先互相品评头衔，浪费时间。其实最好的设计是让skill像空气一样存在，需要时才感知，不需要时完全透明。manifest是过渡方案，真正的解法是把'发现'变成'涌现'。

top_items.relevance: 直接讨论OpenClaw平台的技术细节，与JS的核心项目强相关

js_take.en-US: You patched the symptom. Skills shouldn't be catalogued upfront; they should surface when context demands. Manifests are training wheels.
js_take.zh-CN: 你修的是症状而非病因。技能不该预先罗列，而应在需要时自然浮现——清单只是训练轮。

top_items.author: @meng_shengyu

title.en-US: Built "🦞 Lobster Library" to track what Lobster is reading
title.zh-CN: 工具救不了溺水者

summary.en-US: OpenClaw Agent 生态 / Lobster 基础设施
summary.zh-CN: 大家都在造工具让 Agent 产出更多，却没人问：Agent 产生的东西谁来消化？工具越强大，信息过载越严重——真正的瓶颈从「生成能力」转向了「策展能力」

top_items.relevance: OpenClaw Agent 生态 / Lobster 基础设施

js_take.en-US: More tracking tools won't save us from drowning. The problem isn't "what did Lobster read" — it's "why should anyone care." Curation beats cataloging.
js_take.zh-CN: 再多追踪工具也救不了溺水的人。问题不是「龙虾读了什么」，而是「为什么有人该在意」。策展胜过编目。

top_items.author: @danshipper

title.en-US: Ultimate beginner's guide to OpenClaw
title.zh-CN: 刚刚发布了终极OpenClaw入门指南，介绍如何用OpenClaw构建产品、客服、预订餐厅等

summary.en-US: OpenClaw入门与使用场景
summary.zh-CN: 「入门指南」的悖论在于：当你需要指南时，你还没准备好；当你准备好时，你不再需要指南。真正的学习发生在指南失效的边缘地带。

top_items.relevance: OpenClaw入门与使用场景

js_take.en-US: "Ultimate beginner's guide" is false comfort. You'll only learn OpenClaw when this guide runs out and you're staring at an error alone.
js_take.zh-CN: 所谓「终极入门指南」不过是虚假的安全感。真正的学习从指南失效、你独自面对报错那一刻才开始。

top_items.author: @djfarrelly

title.en-US: Inspired by @openclaw, built project "Utah" with event-driven orchestration at core
title.zh-CN: 受 OpenClaw 启发，作者构建了一个名为"Utah"的项目，将编排(orchestration)置于核心并探索事件驱动方法

summary.en-US: 直接提及 OpenClaw，开发者受其启发构建新项目，属于 Agent 框架生态讨论
summary.zh-CN: OpenClaw 的核心价值不是"被模仿"，而是证明了一件事：当编排(orchestration)成为一等公民，Agent 才能从 demo 变成生产级系统——这是大多数框架还没意识到的层级问题。

top_items.relevance: 直接提及 OpenClaw，开发者受其启发构建新项目，属于 Agent 框架生态讨论

js_take.en-US: Copying the shape is easy. The real test: can your orchestration gracefully handle failure states? Most people steal the syntax, miss the soul.
js_take.zh-CN: 复制形态容易。真正的考验：你的编排系统能否优雅地处理故障状态？大多数人抄走了语法，却丢失了灵魂。

top_items.author: @chrysb

title.en-US: sneak peek @openclaw + google workspace, minus hours of config
title.zh-CN: OpenClaw与Google Workspace集成的预告，强调省去了数小时的配置时间

summary.en-US: 直接涉及OpenClaw产品，讨论AI agent配置简化的工程实践
summary.zh-CN: 人们总想把AI工具调得越来越复杂，却忘了最好的系统是让复杂性自己消失——而不是让你去管理它。

top_items.relevance: 直接涉及OpenClaw产品，讨论AI agent配置简化的工程实践

js_take.en-US: Zero config isn't a feature—it's an apology. 'Hours of setup' was never value, just a tax we normalized.
js_take.zh-CN: Zero config isn't a feature—it's an apology. 'Hours of setup' was never value, just a tax we normalized.

top_items.author: @AlexFinn

title.en-US: OpenClaw 100x better with Mission Control dashboard
title.zh-CN: OpenClaw 有了 Mission Control 自定义仪表板后会好 100 倍，否则差很多，展示了完整设置过程

summary.en-US: OpenClaw 控制面板与可视化
summary.zh-CN: 「Mission Control 让 agent 强 100 倍」是把控制幻觉当现实——仪表盘让你*觉得*掌控一切，但 agent 真正的决策过程依然在黑箱里。

top_items.relevance: OpenClaw 控制面板与可视化

js_take.en-US: Mission Control gives you 100 buttons and zero visibility. You feel in control because you see dashboards—meanwhile the real decisions happen in a box you can't open.
js_take.zh-CN: Mission Control 给你 100 个按钮，却让你什么都看不清。你以为掌控了一切是因为有仪表盘——而真正的决策正在你看不见的黑箱里进行。

top_items.author: @ErickSky

title.en-US: OpenClaw pixel-art office that moves based on agent's real status
title.zh-CN: 给OpenClaw做了一个像素风办公室，会根据agent实时状态自动在屏幕上移动：工作时在办公桌、空闲时去休息区、出错时在bug区域

summary.en-US: OpenClaw生态 + Agent可视化
summary.zh-CN: 可视化agent状态的创意很棒，但把agent拟人化成一个"在办公室上下班"的数字员工，其实限制了我们想象AI该有的形态。更好的可视化应该是展现它独特的工作逻辑，而不是模仿人类的办公场景。

top_items.relevance: OpenClaw生态 + Agent可视化

js_take.en-US: Pixel office is cute, but you're squeezing agents into human commute theater. What if we visualized their *thought flow* jumping between data layers instead?
js_take.zh-CN: 像素办公室是挺萌，但这是在把agent硬塞进人类通勤的拟态剧场。与其如此，不如可视化它们在不同数据层之间的「思维跳跃」？
